description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden sailing ship with a single tall mast and a streamlined hull. The hull is constructed from planks with a natural wood finish, featuring a pointed bow and a squared-off stern. The deck is open and relatively flat, with minimal superstructure and a few small fixtures visible. Rigging lines extend from the mast to the bow and stern, suggesting a traditional sailing configuration. The ship lacks visible cabins or enclosed spaces, emphasizing its open-deck design. No registration markings or decorative elements are present, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
description: A medium-sized wooden sailing ship with a single mast, open deck, and streamlined hull, finished in natural wood tones.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden hull, single mast, and rigging lines extending fore and aft.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the pointed bow, narrow hull profile, and central mast with cross spars.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the hull's shape, mast, and rigging, similar to view 0 but from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the squared-off stern, open deck, and the alignment of the mast and rigging.
attribute_hull_design: The hull is a single displacement type with a pointed bow and squared stern, constructed from wooden planks. There are no visible keels, bulbous bows, or catamaran features. The hull sides are slightly flared, and the deck is open and flat.
attribute_superstructure_layout: The superstructure is minimal, consisting of an open deck with a single central mast and a few small deck fixtures. There are no visible cabins, bridges, or enclosed spaces.
